description_long: This is a classic propeller-driven airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a rounded nose, featuring a pair of straight, low-mounted wings extending from either side. The aircraft is painted primarily white with blue accents and has visible passenger windows along the fuselage, suggesting it is designed for transport. The twin engines are mounted on the wings, each with a prominent propeller, and the landing gear is visible beneath the wings and tail. The tail section features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, with the registration markings visible on the tail fin. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the pilots. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century passenger or cargo aircraft, suitable for both urban airports and regional airfields.
description: A white and blue twin-propeller airplane with low-mounted straight wings, a conventional tail, and a forward cockpit with multiple windows.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full fuselage, passenger windows, wing-mounted engines, and the tail section with registration markings.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the straight wingspan, twin engines, and the conventional tail assembly.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the fuselage, cockpit, wing-mounted engines, and tail, mirroring view 0.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the rounded nose, cockpit windows, propellers, and the symmetrical wing layout.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets; slight dihedral angle present.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main landing gear under the wings and a single tail wheel; fixed gear visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
